stories.headline: PJM floats curtailing power to new data centers, citing 'unsustainable stress' on grid
stories.summary: PJM's 'Powering Reliability Through Market Design' paper warns that data center load growth, plant retirements, and permitting delays may force the RTO to curtail electricity to new large-load customers during peak demand — Western Hub cleared $26.90/MWh and load hit 84.7 GW in this morning's real-time snapshot.

stories.headline: PJM gets 811 generation project applications totaling 220 GW, led by gas and storage
stories.summary: Developers submitted 811 projects to PJM's new interconnection cycle by the April 27 close — 105.8 GW natural gas, 66.5 GW storage, 17.9 GW nuclear, 14.8 GW solar — driven by data center demand; PJM now begins validation, with no backlog from prior queues.

stories.headline: Shapiro quietly working to derail AOC-backed PA-03 candidate ahead of May 19 primary
stories.summary: Axios reports Gov. Josh Shapiro is trying to block a left-wing congressional candidate championed by Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez in the May 19 PA-03 Democratic primary, framed as an early 2028-presidential proxy fight; Chris Rabb, Sharif Street, and Ala Stanford are now flooding Philly airwaves.

stories.headline: Shapiro tells PA Senate to 'come back to work' and pass a budget
stories.summary: Speaking in Lancaster County Friday, Gov. Shapiro called on the GOP-controlled Senate — not scheduled to reconvene until June 1 — to return and 'get a budget to my desk as soon as possible,' siding with Sens. Costa and Hughes who said Republicans were 'wasting time.'

stories.headline: Shapiro endorses Bucks County's Bob Harvie in PA-01
stories.summary: Gov. Shapiro on Sunday formally endorsed Bucks County Commissioner Bob Harvie in the 1st Congressional District; Harvie also picked up a New Democrat Coalition endorsement and is one of three Dems the group is backing against GOP incumbents.

stories.headline: Johnson, Jeffries enter two-week stretch with shifting fortunes after SCOTUS VRA ruling
stories.summary: Punchbowl: Supreme Court rulings in Washington and Richmond reshape the 2026 House map, with up to 10 seats potentially affected — Tennessee Republicans already eliminated Rep. Steve Cohen's seat, Louisiana set to gain at least one GOP seat, and Alabama could yield two more if SCOTUS clears the way.

stories.headline: Schumer turns $1B Trump ballroom security ask into reconciliation flashpoint
stories.summary: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer's Dear Colleague letter Monday morning tees up Byrd Rule challenges and forced amendment votes on the $72B ICE/border reconciliation bill ahead of a planned floor vote the week of May 18; moderate Republicans are privately objecting.

stories.headline: Trump heads to China Tuesday seeking Xi's help on Iran war
stories.summary: President Trump travels to Beijing Tuesday for his first in-person Xi meeting in nearly seven months, bringing a group of CEOs and pushing for progress on the Iran war and reopening the Strait of Hormuz; Trump rejected an Iranian peace offer Sunday.

stories.headline: Senate teed up for cloture vote on Warsh as Fed governor Monday evening
stories.summary: Senate returns at 3 p.m. for two roll-call votes at 5:30 p.m. — a procedural vote on executive nominees and a cloture vote on Kevin Warsh's nomination to the Federal Reserve Board; a confirmation vote on Warsh as Fed chair follows later this week, with Powell's term ending Friday.

stories.headline: Pirates blow late lead, fall 7-6 in 12 to Giants on dropped fly
stories.summary: Bubba Chandler went 5 IP / 2 ER and Konnor Griffin and Oneil Cruz homered, but the Giants tied it and won 7-6 in 12 innings Sunday after RF Ryan O'Hearn dropped a Matt Chapman fly, sending the automatic runner to third; Pirates fall to 22-19, host Colorado Tuesday-Thursday.

stories.headline: Steelers wrap rookie minicamp; OTAs open May 18
stories.summary: Pittsburgh held its 2026 rookie minicamp this past weekend, with first-round OT Max Iheanachor (Arizona State, No. 21), 2nd-round WR Germie Bernard (Alabama), and 3rd-round QB Drew Allar (Penn State) on the field; OTAs begin May 18 and run through June 12.
